name: Financial Services (Zimbabwe)
definition: Regulated and allied financial businesses: banks and deposit-takers, microfinance, insurance and pensions, securities and asset management, payments and money-transfer services, and financial advisory intermediaries.

subSectors.name: Banking and deposit-taking
subSectors.description: Commercial banks, building societies and deposit-taking institutions.

subSectors.name: Insurance and pensions
subSectors.description: Insurers, reinsurers, brokers, agents and pension funds.

subSectors.name: Securities and asset management
subSectors.description: Brokers, asset managers and collective investment schemes.

subSectors.name: Payments and money transfer
subSectors.description: Payment providers, money-transfer agencies and bureaux de change.

typicalBusinessSizes.description: Established insurers, MFIs and asset managers.

officialSizeThresholds.note: No official institution-size classification or capital-threshold table was verified from an official source in this revision; minimum capital requirements are deliberately not restated.

notes: Descriptive segmentation for product design. Institution licensing classes and prudential obligations are declared as research gaps; VAKA's ERP scope here is business administration, not prudential systems.
